name: History Taking — General

anchors.9-10: No errors; native-like medical register throughout
anchors.7-8: Occasional minor errors; fully intelligible; appropriate register
anchors.5-6: Some structural errors requiring listener effort
anchors.1-2: Very frequent errors; comprehension significantly impaired

anchors.9-10: All medical terminology and clinical facts correct
anchors.5-6: Some incorrect terminology or clinical reasoning

anchors.9-10: Clear differential verbalized; dangerous diagnosis prioritized; systematic approach
anchors.7-8: Reasonable differential implied; key risk factors explored
anchors.5-6: Some structured reasoning but key items missed

anchors.9-10: Smooth, natural pacing; appropriate signposting; no disruptive hesitation
anchors.1-2: Communication severely disrupted by fluency issues

anchors.9-10: Exceptional empathy and professionalism; creates psychological safety
anchors.7-8: Good empathy; acknowledges emotion; polite and professional
anchors.5-6: Adequate but mechanical empathy; some emotional cues missed
anchors.3-4: Little empathic response; information delivery prioritized
anchors.1-2: No empathic response; dismissive or unprofessional

checklist.item: used Teach-back method to verify patient understanding

empathy_markers: used patient's name at least once
appropriate pause/space given for patient emotion
